Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

PLAN-170: Require Ollama for OpenCode mode

OpenCode mode now enforces local Ollama for adversarial auditing
with no fallback to Claude subagent. This ensures audit independence.

Changes:
- Add AuditRequiredError exception for enforcement
- Add detect_opencode_context() and detect_context_mode() functions
- Modify get_audit_backend() to raise in OpenCode when Ollama unavailable
- Add context_mode and enforcement fields to get_backend_status()
- Add 11 new tests for OpenCode detection and enforcement
- Fix test isolation issue with backend cache fixture

Context detection order:
1. CRUX_TOOL env var (explicit)
2. ~/.config/crux/setup-state/selected_tool file
3. OPENCODE_SESSION / CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRY_POINT env vars
4. Default: Claude Code (graceful fallback for unknown)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix report editor: fuzzy matching, full report context, streaming edits

The "Edit with Claude" overlay had two core problems: edits silently
failed due to exact-match-only SEARCH blocks, and large reports only
got a summary sent to the model (starving it of content).

- Add fuzzy whitespace matching fallback in applySearchReplace so
  minor whitespace differences no longer cause silent failures
- Always send full report text to the model (remove Mode 2/3 partial
  context), ensuring exact text is available for SEARCH blocks
- Apply SEARCH/REPLACE blocks incrementally during streaming for
  real-time preview updates instead of waiting for full response
- Enhance system prompt with clinical depth instructions and stricter
  verbatim-copy rules for SEARCH blocks

commit.message:
Remove unused size_exceeded field and InvalidResponse variant

size_exceeded is no longer used after get_data() was simplified to skip
any file with absent contents. InvalidResponse was only used by the
removed validate() method. Drop both to keep the build warning-free.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add file locking to prevent hook race conditions (#46)

* fix: add file locking to prevent hook race conditions

Concurrent hook processes (e.g. PreToolUse + PostToolUse firing
simultaneously) could race on the session file: both read the old
state, apply changes independently, and the last writer clobbers
the first writer's changes. This was particularly visible with
subagent tracking where SubagentStart entries were immediately
overwritten by concurrent PreToolUse hooks.

Wrap the entire read-modify-write cycle in an exclusive flock on
a .lock file alongside the session file. Falls back to unlocked
operation if the lock file can't be created.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: include Xcode version in DerivedData cache key

The cache key only hashed xcodeproj files, not the Xcode version.
When the runner's Xcode gets updated, stale .pcm files cause
"module.modulemap has been modified" build failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* review: simplify lock, move cleanup outside lock, clean up .lock files

- Simplify withSessionLock to single body() call using flag pattern
- Move cleanupSessionsForProject outside the lock (scans all sessions,
  doesn't need serialization with current session's write)
- Clean up orphaned .lock files when sessions are removed (both in
  HookHandler.removeSession and SessionManager.archiveAndRemoveDeadSessions)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* review: fail hard if lock cannot be acquired

Remove silent fallback — if we allow proceeding without the lock,
the lock doesn't actually fix the race condition. The only realistic
failure is open() failing (disk full, permissions), which means
writeToFile would fail too.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Fix update check: use semver comparison to prevent downgrades

The update check was using a simple != comparison, which triggered
"updates" when an agent was running a version ahead of the latest
GitHub release (e.g. manually installed). Replace with proper semver
comparison so updates only fire when the release is strictly newer.

commit.message:
Fix crown scalp showing on all hair styles, make ponytail high

All hair paths used y=22 (exact head top) but quadratic beziers
cut inside the ellipse at off-center points. Overshoot to y=20
so hair fully covers the crown. Also thickened inner returns.

Ponytail redesigned as high ponytail — starts from crown, arcs
up to y=6 before cascading down to y=80. Visually distinct from
short crop.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(compiler): classify component props as reactive sources [#964] (#973)

* fix(compiler): classify component props as reactive sources [#964]

The ReactivityAnalyzer now treats component props (both named and
destructured) as reactive sources, matching the existing treatment
of useContext results. This causes const declarations derived from
props to be wrapped in computed(), so derived values update when
getter-backed props change.

Handles named params (props.title), destructured bindings ({ title }),
aliases ({ id: cardId }), and skips rest patterns (...rest) which
are plain object snapshots.

Closes #964

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(compiler): add edge case tests for props reactivity [#964]

Add tests from adversarial review:
- Arrow function components (named and destructured props)
- Props with default values
- Callback consts derived from props (documents current behavior, see #978)
- Props used only in JSX (no unnecessary computeds)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(compiler): restrict props reactivity to conventional param names [#964]

Only treat named parameters as reactive props when the name is literally
"props" or "__props". Factory functions in ui-primitives use "options" or
"config" as their parameter name — these must NOT be classified as
reactive sources, otherwise consts like JSX elements get wrapped in
computed() and break at runtime.

Fixes @vertz/ui-primitives#test CI failure where AlertDialogRoot's
trigger/overlay/content elements were wrapped in ComputedImpl.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat: suporte a aulas repetidas em dias diferentes (mesma aula pedagógica)

- Nova coluna aula_mae_id em aulas_virtuais para vincular repetições
- Trigger criar_aulas_diario_automatico: pula repetições (não cria entrada duplicada no diário) e remove texto automático do campo observacoes
- Trigger sincronizar_presenca_diario: usa COALESCE(aula_mae_id, aula_id) para sincronizar presença de qualquer sessão para o diário da aula mãe
- RPC update_aula_virtual_safe: inclui migração retroativa ao definir aula_mae_id pela primeira vez (migra presenças e remove entrada duplicada do diário)
- useDiario.ts: cálculo de frequência agrupado por unidade pedagógica (N sessões = 1 aula)
- AulaVirtualForm e AulaVirtualEditForm: toggle "Repetição de outra aula" + select da aula original

OCAD audit fixes: accessibility, dead CSS cleanup, vertical rhythm, typography

- Badge link contrast: --text-soft → --amber (WCAG AA pass)
- Global focus-visible styles with clip-path-safe inset offsets
- prefers-reduced-motion: disable particles and badge blink
- Remove dead logo CSS (.l-frame, .l-brace, .l-tsa, .l-tsa-bare)
- Logo.tsx: #C084FC → var(--particle) everywhere
- Badge tooltip: z-index 100→50, remove clipped box-shadow
- Section padding standardized: 72px standard, 96px anchored
- .dir-text 15px→14px to match .step-body

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: --dry-run flag, status command, global flag consolidation (#53)

* feat: add --dry-run flag, status command, and global flag consolidation (#50, #51, #52)

- Extract GLOBAL_ARGS (quiet, dryRun) and FORMAT_ARG (format) into
  cli-args.ts; replace per-command definitions with spread
- Add --dry-run to all commands: validates args and shows planned
  action without Chrome connection
- Add `cavendish status` command: checks CDP connectivity, ChatGPT
  login state, profile directory, and config file existence
- Export CDP/path constants from browser-manager.ts; status.ts reuses
  them instead of duplicating

Closes #50, closes #51, closes #52

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address CodeRabbit review findings for status command

- Include HTTP status in ChatGPT check failure messages
- Capture error details in catch blocks instead of generic messages
- Output text-format status to stderr (stdout reserved for JSON data)
- Include config.ok in exit code check (all checks must pass)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: exclude config.ok from exit code check

config.json is informational only — no code path creates it yet.
Including it in the exit code would make `status` always fail on
fresh installations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: send text status output to stdout for consistency

All other commands send --format text output to stdout via text().
Align status command with this convention so piping works:
  cavendish status --format text | grep CDP

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: tighten ChatGPT login detection to exclude shared links

Filter out /share/ URLs from login detection so that only
authenticated ChatGPT tabs (not shared conversation links)
are treated as logged-in indicators.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: treat missing ChatGPT tab as ok (login status unknown)

When no ChatGPT tab is open, report ok: true with an informational
message instead of failing. The ask command opens tabs on demand,
so a missing tab is not a real failure — only a detected auth page
should cause exit code 1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: treat shared-link-only ChatGPT tabs as ok

When only /share/ URLs are open (no authenticated or auth pages),
return ok: true instead of ok: false. Like the "no tab" case,
shared links don't indicate a login failure — login status is
simply unknown.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: surface error details in CDP connection check

Capture the error in checkCdp's catch block and include it in the
detail message, matching the pattern used in checkChatGPT. This
helps distinguish connection refused, timeout, and DNS errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: validate chat ID before dry-run in all commands

Add assertValidChatId() before the dry-run check in read, delete,
archive, and move commands. This ensures malformed chat IDs are
rejected in both dry-run and real execution modes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
refactor: 일정 상세 팝업 버튼 전면 개선 (UI/UX 설계 적용)

## 변경 사항

### 1. 버튼 사이즈 축소 ✓
- padding: 14px 16px → 8px 18px (기본 btn 스타일로 복원)
- font-size: 16px → 14px
- font-weight: 700 → 500
- 결과: 컴팩트한 버튼 UI (이전 상태로 복원)

### 2. 버튼 순서 변경 ✓
- 변경 전: [닫기] [수정] [삭제]
- 변경 후: [삭제] [수정] [닫기]
- 이유: 파괴적 액션(삭제)을 명확히 좌측 배치

### 3. 삭제 버튼 동적 처리 ✓
- 문구 축약: "정말 삭제하시겠습니까?" → "삭제하시겠어요?"
- 동작:
  * 초기: [삭제] [수정] [닫기] (모두 auto 크기)
  * 클릭 후: [삭제하시겠어요?──] [수정] [닫기]
    (삭제 버튼만 flex:1로 확대, 수정/닫기는 우측 고정)
- 애니메이션: transition: flex 0.2s ease

### 4. 반응형 대응 ✓
- 모바일 화면에서도 문구 전체 표시 (flex 자동 조절)
- 극소형 화면(320px)에서도 안전한 렌더링

## 기술 세부사항

**ScheduleDetail.jsx**
- 버튼 JSX 순서 재정렬: [삭제] [수정] [닫기]
- 동적 클래스: btn btn-danger${confirmDelete ? ' btn-danger-confirm' : ''}

**ScheduleDetail.css**
- align-items: stretch → center (높이 통일)
- gap: 12px → 8px (간격 축소)
- 삭제(확인): .btn-danger-confirm { flex: 1; }
- 닫기: .btn-secondary { margin-left: auto; }

**global.css**
- 변경 없음 (기본 .btn 스타일 유지)

## 에이전트 팀 검토
- Designer 팀에서 UI/UX 설계 완료
- 반응형 대응 및 레이아웃 검증 완료
- 모든 버튼 조합(canDelete/canEdit) 대응 확인

commit.message:
Fix fullscreen image viewer cropping

Restructure the fullscreen modal to use flex column layout so the image
area and thumbnail strip don't overlap. Use 100dvh for proper mobile
viewport, remove border/padding from dialog, hide the duplicate default
close button, and move thumbnails to a non-overlapping bottom bar.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: install @nestjs/cli globally in API builder to avoid workspace CWD issues

pnpm build from within a workspace member calls nest build but pnpm
workspace resolution doesn't reliably set CWD for the CLI. Installing
@nestjs/cli globally and calling nest build directly from WORKDIR
/app/apps/api guarantees tsconfig.json is found.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
